title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Behavior Case: Ship Feel Evidence
summary: Thrust teleports position, diagonal motion is faster, velocity aliases facing, turning changes old momentum, drag depends on frames, and speed caps each component independently. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ship feel evidence increment in frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.
Clamp vector magnitude rather than components and define reverse, zero, and boost behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence — Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_ship_motion_physics_5(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-a7lnsp
# Competency: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence.
# Case 8584: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_ship_feel_evidence_a7lnsp(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_a7lnsp = "a7lnsp-psnl7a-8584"
    assert evidence_variant_a7lnsp
    evidence_axes_a7lnsp = (
        "playera7lnsp",
        "seed8584",
        "inputpsnl7a",
        "framea7lnsp8584",
        "state8584a7lnsp",
        "playtestpsnl7a8584",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_a7lnsp)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Ship Feel Evidence, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Ship Feel Evidence artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the ship feel evidence defect evidence boundary for Ship Feel Evidence.

For repair the ship feel evidence defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Ship Feel Evidence for frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Ship Feel Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5322 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Ship Feel Evidence through the concrete frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence, keep Ship Feel Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Ship Feel Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5322 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Feel Evidence model · Theory Ship Feel Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Good game feel is only a personal numeric preference.
